texto: A woman is entitled to her secrets. She should be able to hide them away for as long as she wants, without people constantly prying, trying to take a peek inside her head. But secrets are exhausting and that's the plain truth of the thing. The effort of keeping them locked away, shielding them from view......I'm only human, after all. I look away from him, though, because it is the only way I can do this.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Sheena Kamal sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “A woman is entitled to her secrets. She should be able to hide them away for as long as she wants…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, eyes-like-mine, thriller, útil em momentos de thriller.

frase_original: A woman is entitled to her secrets. She should be able to hide them away for as long as she wants, without people constantly prying, trying to take a peek inside her head. But secrets are exhausting and that's the plain truth of the thing. The effort of keeping them locked away, shielding them from view......I'm only human, after all. I look away from him, though, because it is the only way I can do this.

texto: I'd told him what it had been like, living on thew streets. How the police were to be avoided at all costs, how they never helped you if you were homeless or busking. How they pushed you around and made you leave public spaces. How they let other people treat you like garbage without intervening. Cops would never support someone like me. Never.' - Eyes Like Mine
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sheena Kamal: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I'd told him what it had been like, living on thew streets. How the police were to be avoided at …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, eyes-like-mine, thriller pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de thriller.
